page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-8 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with multithreading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

Performance benchmarks place the c2d-standard-8 in the average tier for most multi-core and memory operations, while demonstrating strong single-core CPU performance. In stress-ng testing, single-core CPU performance ranks in the top 25% with a score of 2865.09, whereas multi-core performance falls in the middle 50% at 11559.23. Memory bandwidth tests show average throughput, reaching 553.27 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 46.79 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Computational tasks such as software compilation and ray tracing also align with mid-range performance expectations.

From a resource density perspective, the c2d-standard-8 offers a balanced hardware profile suitable for workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation without the premium cost of high-memory or GPU-accelerated instances. The lack of local storage and public IPv4 addresses out of the box means storage and networking costs are externalized. This configuration is well-suited for general-purpose compute tasks, web serving, software compilation, Redis-based caching, and lightweight LLM inference using small models.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-8 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 4 physical cores and 2 threads per core. This instance does not include bundled GPUs or local storage. Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% in stress-ng tests, while multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average tier. With a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per vCPU and dedicated CPU allocation, the c2d-standard-8 provides a stable environment for mid-range compute workloads. It is suited for web serving, software compilation, Redis caching, and lightweight text processing tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-8 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor, delivering strong single-core performance for web serving and compilation.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-8 is an x86_64 VM with 8 vCPUs and 32 GB RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU for general compute workloads.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC virtual machine delivering strong single-core performance and balanced memory allocation for general-purpose compute workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores
Equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per vCPU)
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators included
Well-suited for web serving, software compilation, and Redis caching
